title: Retrieval Routing Policy
text: Lexical or exact identifier questions should route to BM25. Semantic questions should route to dense retrieval.

title: Identifier Guide
text: Exact identifiers, codes, and section references are best handled by sparse lexical retrieval.

title: Mammal Facts
text: A mammal is a vertebrate animal. Bats are mammals and can fly.

title: PRISM Policy
text: Property and membership questions should route to the KG backend. Multi-hop questions should route to hybrid.

title: PRISM Overview
text: PRISM is a multi-representation retrieval router that compares BM25, dense, KG, and hybrid backends.

title: HIPAA 45 CFR 164.510
text: HIPAA section 164.510 covers uses and disclosures requiring an opportunity for the individual to agree or object.

title: HIPAA 45 CFR 164.512
text: HIPAA section 164.512 covers uses and disclosures for which an authorization or opportunity to agree or object is not required.

title: HIPAA 45 CFR 164.514
text: HIPAA section 164.514 covers other requirements relating to uses and disclosures of protected health information.

title: HTML aria-label
text: The aria-label attribute defines an accessible name for an element when no visible label is available.

title: HTML aria-labelledby
text: The aria-labelledby attribute identifies elements that label the current element.

title: ICD-10-CM J18.0
text: ICD-10-CM code J18.0 identifies bronchopneumonia, unspecified organism.

title: ICD-10-CM J18.1
text: ICD-10-CM code J18.1 identifies lobar pneumonia, unspecified organism.

title: ICD-10-CM J18.9
text: ICD-10-CM code J18.9 identifies pneumonia, unspecified organism.

title: numpy.linalg.norm
text: numpy.linalg.norm returns a matrix or vector norm, with behavior controlled by ord and axis.

title: numpy.linalg.svd
text: numpy.linalg.svd performs singular value decomposition on an array.

title: PostgreSQL jsonb_insert
text: PostgreSQL jsonb_insert inserts a new value into a JSONB document at a specified path.

title: PostgreSQL jsonb_set
text: PostgreSQL jsonb_set updates or inserts a value at a specified JSONB path.

title: RFC-7230 HTTP/1.1 Message Syntax and Routing
text: RFC-7230 defines HTTP/1.1 message syntax, routing, connection management, and related requirements.

title: RFC-7231 HTTP/1.1 Semantics and Content
text: RFC-7231 defines HTTP/1.1 semantics and content, including methods, status codes, and content negotiation.

title: RFC-7235 HTTP/1.1 Authentication
text: RFC-7235 defines the HTTP/1.1 authentication framework and related status codes.

title: 42 U.S.C. §1981
text: 42 U.S.C. §1981 protects equal rights under the law to make and enforce contracts.

title: 42 U.S.C. §1983
text: 42 U.S.C. §1983 provides a civil action for deprivation of rights under color of state law.

title: 42 U.S.C. §1985
text: 42 U.S.C. §1985 concerns conspiracies to interfere with civil rights.

title: sklearn.feature_extraction.text.CountVectorizer
text: CountVectorizer converts text documents to a matrix of token counts.

title: sklearn.feature_extraction.text.TfidfVectorizer
text: TfidfVectorizer converts a collection of raw documents to a matrix of TF-IDF features.

title: torch.nn.BCELoss
text: torch.nn.BCELoss measures binary cross entropy between target and input probabilities.

title: torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss
text: torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss computes cross entropy loss between input logits and target class indices or probabilities.

title: torch.nn.NLLLoss
text: torch.nn.NLLLoss computes the negative log likelihood loss and is commonly paired with LogSoftmax.

title: HIPAA 164.512 meeting notes
text: HIPAA 164.512 is mentioned next to 164.510 and 164.514 in compliance training notes without the authoritative answer.

title: ARIA naming overview
text: aria-label and aria-labelledby both appear in accessibility guidance about element names.

title: J18.9 billing memo
text: J18.9, J18.0, and J18.1 appear in a billing memo with mixed pneumonia examples and no definitive mapping.

title: JSONB function comparison
text: PostgreSQL jsonb_set and jsonb_insert both manipulate JSONB paths, making this a near-match distractor.

title: Numpy linear algebra quick notes
text: numpy.linalg.norm and numpy.linalg.svd are both named in generic notes about array computations.

title: RFC-7231 errata discussion
text: RFC-7231 appears in an errata note that also references RFC-7230 and RFC-7235, but this note does not define HTTP semantics.

title: RFC-7321 distractor
text: RFC-7321 is a nearby-looking identifier used here as a lexical distractor for RFC-7231.

title: Civil rights sections overview
text: Sections §1981, §1983, and §1985 are listed together in a broad overview with similar civil-rights language.

title: Sklearn vectorizer comparison
text: TfidfVectorizer and CountVectorizer are compared in a tutorial with overlapping vocabulary and code fragments.

title: Cross entropy blog
text: A blog compares torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss, torch.nn.NLLLoss, and torch.nn.BCELoss but gives informal advice rather than API definitions.

title: Animal class bridge clutter
text: Birds, mammals, fish, amphibians, insects, vertebrates, and animals appear in a mixed taxonomy paragraph.

title: Bat vertebrate mixed passage
text: Bats, birds, and insects are discussed together. The passage mentions vertebrate animals, mammals, wings, flight, and mosquitoes in a cluttered order.

title: Echolocation clutter
text: Dolphins and bats both use echolocation, but the passage mixes property, sensory ability, and mammal facts.

title: Flight capability clutter
text: Bats, eagles, owls, sparrows, and ducks appear near fly, flight, wings, and movement language.

title: Food web clutter
text: Owls, cats, frogs, bats, mice, mosquitoes, and fish appear in a noisy food-web summary with multiple eating relations.

title: Milk relation clutter
text: Mammals produce milk, cows produce milk, whales produce milk, and bats produce milk are all stated in a dense list.

title: Penguin bridge distractor
text: Penguins are birds and birds are vertebrates, but this noisy passage also discusses swimming, wings, and flightlessness.

title: Swimming capability clutter
text: Ducks, penguins, frogs, salmon, sharks, whales, and dolphins are all named near the swim capability.

title: Whale swimming notes
text: Whales swim, produce milk, and are mammals; the passage repeats vertebrate and animal terms without a clean bridge statement.

title: Wings relation clutter
text: Sparrows, ducks, chickens, ostriches, and bats have wings, but not all fly.

title: Semantic distractor 000: Climate Grief
text: Climate grief is sadness about ecological loss; it overlaps with climate anxiety but emphasizes mourning rather than anxious anticipation.

title: Semantic distractor 001: Test Anxiety
text: Test anxiety is worry before exams and can sound like climate anxiety in abstract discussions of stress.

title: Semantic distractor 002: Plant Respiration
text: Plant respiration consumes stored sugars and releases energy, often confused with photosynthesis in short explanations.

title: Semantic distractor 003: Solar Panels
text: Solar panels convert sunlight into electricity, a semantically similar but non-biological energy conversion process.

title: Semantic distractor 004: Organizational Diffusion
text: Organizational diffusion describes spread of practices in firms and can distract from diffusion of innovations.

title: Semantic distractor 005: Heat Stress
text: Heat stress describes physiological strain from high temperatures, related to but not identical with urban heat island.

title: Semantic distractor 006: Sleep Hygiene
text: Sleep hygiene improves rest and memory but is not the same concept as memory consolidation.

title: Semantic distractor 007: Autoimmune Disease
text: Autoimmune disease involves immune response but is not simply an allergy to harmless substances.

title: Semantic distractor 008: Fungal Pathogens
text: Fungal pathogens can harm plants, unlike mycorrhizal networks that often exchange nutrients.

title: Semantic distractor 009: Data Drift
text: Data drift changes model inputs over time and can be confused with algorithmic bias.

title: Semantic distractor 010: Jet Lag
text: Jet lag disrupts daily timing but is not the underlying circadian rhythm itself.

title: Semantic distractor 011: Cap And Trade
text: Cap and trade is one policy instrument related to carbon pricing but not the general concept.

title: Semantic distractor 012: Memoir
text: A memoir is a written life story, related to but narrower than narrative identity.

title: Semantic distractor 013: Open Data Portal
text: An open data portal can be part of digital commons but lacks the broader governance concept.

title: Semantic distractor 014: Weather Forecast
text: Weather forecasts mention rain and evaporation but do not define the water cycle.

title: Semantic distractor 015: Supervised Learning
text: Supervised learning uses labeled examples rather than rewards and penalties.

title: Semantic distractor 016: Word Embeddings
text: Word embeddings are one implementation related to distributional semantics.

title: Semantic distractor 017: Multitasking
text: Multitasking divides attention but is broader than notification-driven attention fragmentation.

title: Semantic distractor 018: Habitat
text: A habitat is where an organism lives, while ecological niche includes role and interactions.

title: Semantic distractor 019: Standpoint Theory
text: Standpoint theory overlaps with situated knowledge but is a more specific theoretical frame.

title: Bat flight relation
text: The bat to fly relation is supported by the KG fact that bat is capable of fly and text notes about bat flight.

title: Bat mosquito relation
text: The bat to mosquito relation is dietary: bat eats mosquito in the demo KG.

title: Bat to vertebrate bridge
text: A bat connects to vertebrate through the mammal class: bat is a mammal, and mammal is a vertebrate.

title: Bird vertebrate relation
text: The bird to vertebrate relation is class inheritance: bird is a vertebrate.

title: Cat mouse relation
text: The cat to mouse relation is dietary: cat eats mouse in the demo KG.

title: Dolphin echolocation relation
text: The dolphin to echolocation relation is a property relation: dolphin has property echolocation.

title: Duck swim relation
text: The duck to swim relation is capability based: duck is capable of swim.

title: Eagle fish relation
text: The eagle to fish relation is dietary: eagle eats fish in the demo KG.

title: Frog mosquito relation
text: The frog to mosquito relation is dietary: frog eats mosquito in the demo KG.

title: Insect animal relation
text: The insect to animal relation is class inheritance: insect is an animal.

title: Mammal milk relation
text: The mammal to milk relation is production: mammal produces milk in the demo KG.

title: Mammal vertebrate relation
text: The mammal to vertebrate relation is class inheritance: mammal is a vertebrate.

title: Mosquito to animal bridge
text: A mosquito connects to animal through insect: mosquito is an insect, and insect is an animal.

title: Owl mouse relation
text: The owl to mouse relation is dietary: owl eats mouse in the demo KG.

title: Penguin swim relation
text: The penguin to swim relation is capability: penguin is capable of swim in the demo KG.

title: Penguin to vertebrate bridge
text: A penguin connects to vertebrate through bird: penguin is a bird, and bird is a vertebrate.

title: Salmon to vertebrate bridge
text: A salmon connects to vertebrate through fish: salmon is a fish, and fish is a vertebrate.

title: Sparrow wings relation
text: The sparrow to wings relation is property based: sparrow has property wings.

title: Whale swim relation
text: The whale to swim relation is capability based: whale is capable of swim.

title: Whale to vertebrate bridge
text: A whale connects to vertebrate through mammal: whale is a mammal, and mammal is a vertebrate.

title: Algorithmic bias
text: Algorithmic bias occurs when data, model design, or deployment context produces unfair or systematically skewed outputs.

title: Allergy
text: An allergy is an immune response in which the body reacts strongly to a usually harmless substance such as pollen or food.

title: Attention fragmentation
text: Attention fragmentation describes reduced sustained focus when notifications, context switching, and interruptions divide cognitive effort.

title: Butterfly effect
text: The butterfly effect names sensitive dependence where tiny changes in initial conditions can produce large later differences.

title: Carbon pricing
text: Carbon pricing uses taxes or markets to attach a cost to greenhouse gas emissions and encourage lower-carbon choices.

title: Circadian rhythm
text: A circadian rhythm is an internal daily timing cycle that helps regulate sleep, hormones, alertness, and metabolism.

title: Circular economy
text: A circular economy designs products and systems to keep materials in use and reduce waste through repair, reuse, and recycling.

title: Climate anxiety
text: Climate anxiety describes distress and worry about climate change, ecological loss, and uncertain planetary futures.

title: Collective intelligence
text: Collective intelligence emerges when groups combine diverse information, coordination, and feedback to solve problems.

title: Diffusion of innovations
text: Diffusion of innovations explains how new ideas, practices, and technologies spread through communities and social systems.

title: Digital commons
text: Digital commons are shared online knowledge resources maintained through community norms, open collaboration, and collective governance.

title: Distributional semantics
text: Distributional semantics represents word meaning through patterns of nearby context and usage across language data.

title: Ecological niche
text: An ecological niche is the role a species occupies in an environment, including resources, interactions, and conditions for survival.

title: Impostor syndrome
text: Impostor syndrome is persistent self-doubt where capable people feel like frauds despite evidence of competence.

title: Memory consolidation
text: Memory consolidation is the process by which experiences and learning become more stable over time, especially during sleep.

title: Mycorrhizal network
text: A mycorrhizal network is a relationship between fungi and plant roots that can move nutrients and chemical signals through soil.

title: Narrative identity
text: Narrative identity is the way people form a sense of self by organizing memories and events into a life story.

title: Photosynthesis
text: Photosynthesis is the biological process where plants and other organisms convert sunlight, carbon dioxide, and water into stored chemical energy.

title: Reinforcement learning
text: Reinforcement learning studies agents that learn actions through rewards, penalties, exploration, and feedback from an environment.

title: Situated knowledge
text: Situated knowledge emphasizes that perspective, social position, and lived context shape what people observe and know.

title: Urban heat island
text: An urban heat island occurs when built surfaces make a city warmer than surrounding rural areas.

title: Water cycle
text: The water cycle describes evaporation, condensation, precipitation, runoff, and storage as water moves through Earth systems.
